title: Add ecosystem fixtures and compatibility tests for Onlystack, FitKind, and Sitches-style configs
description: Prove Sentinel's Atlas-era compatibility model against the products that will adopt it first rather than relying on abstract adapter assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Sentinel has compatibility fixtures representing Onlystack, FitKind, and Sitches-style input shapes
Tests prove the migration path against realistic sample configs rather than only unit-level toy data
Failures point to actionable config or adapter drift instead of generic parse errors
The fixture set is ready to grow with Atlas pilot adopters
proofRequirements: fixture-based tests exist for representative ecosystem samples
docs mention the supported sample-config coverage
nonGoals: embedding full product repos into Sentinel
solving every product-specific route problem inside Sentinel

notes.text: Execution started for ecosystem compatibility fixtures. This slice also corrects the Atlas contract drift so authored local ids and paths are colon-based while filesystem artifact segments use derived safe keys.

Completed ecosystem compatibility coverage by adding Onlystack, FitKind, and Sitches-style sample configs under examples/ecosystem, converting the Atlas sample manifest/session fixtures to colon-based local ids plus derived safe artifact keys, updating compatibility docs, and refreshing proof at ~/Desktop/screenshots/sentinel/atlas-ecosystem-proof.png. Verification passed: npm test, npm run build, git diff --check.
